title: Toolchain Selection, Compatibility, and Reproducible Setup · Risk Brief: Bootstrap Smoke
summary: Select a minimal compatible toolchain and prove clean-machine setup. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates bootstrap smoke increment in a pinned bootstrap and compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test setup, build, and checks from a clean environment.
Keep one canonical config per concern and document generated files.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Predict Bootstrap Smoke
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates toolchain. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates toolchain. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Bootstrap Smoke contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test setup, build, and checks from a clean environment.

content.text: Test setup, build, and checks from a clean environment. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates toolchain. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Bootstrap Smoke, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a pinned bootstrap and compatibility matrix.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: If setup works on the author machine it is documented correctly. This fails the Bootstrap Smoke contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Bootstrap Smoke evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-bootstrap-smoke-1c6bdy: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Bootstrap Smoke into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-bootstrap-smoke-1c6bdy:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the Bootstrap Smoke model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Bootstrap Smoke applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test setup, build, and checks from a clean environment.
